name: gudhi_cascade_persistence
description: Persistent homology over L0-L7 constraint cascade. Detects topological features in family survival/death patterns.

pytorch.reason: tried for tensor ops; numpy sufficient for distance matrices

pyg.reason: graph neural nets not needed for persistence computation

z3.reason: not relevant: no SMT constraints in persistence homology

sympy.reason: not relevant: no symbolic algebra needed

clifford.reason: not relevant: no Clifford algebra ops

geomstats.reason: not relevant: Riemannian geometry not needed

rustworkx.reason: not relevant: GUDHI handles complex construction

toponetx.reason: tried for cell complex comparison; GUDHI simplex tree is better fit

gudhi.reason: primary tool: Rips complex, Alpha complex, persistence diagrams, Betti numbers

positive.rips_H0_detail: At least 2 long-lived H0 components expected (survivors vs killed groups)
positive.rips_L4_detail: Kill events create 3 distinct clusters that merge at filtration 2.0. The existence of multiple merge events proves kills created topological structure.
positive.L4_cluster_detail: All 18 L4-killed families have identical survival vectors -> distance 0
positive.L6_separate_detail: L6-killed should be closer to each other than to L4-killed
positive.betti_H0_at_0_detail: 3 distinct survival profiles = 3 connected components at threshold 0

negative.random_spread_detail: Real cascade has 1 unique death values, random has 2. Random should be more spread.
negative.trivial_detail: All-survive cascade: exactly 1 eternal H0 component, no merges, no loops. This is trivial persistence -- no topological events.

boundary.L8_no_kill_detail: Adding L8 that kills nobody preserves the number of H0 persistence pairs. The topological structure (3 clusters merging) is unchanged, though filtration values shift slightly.
boundary.move_detail: Moving L4 kills to L2 changes Hamming distances between clusters, producing different filtration merge values. This proves layer ordering matters for the topological structure.
boundary.two_family_detail: Two families with Hamming distance 6: should see 2 H0 pairs, one merging at filtration 6.0
